pi_items.source: Path validation - consistent with PowerShell setup script which creates the C:\Users\Docker\Desktop\BlueSkyPlanTasks directory

pi_items.verified_value: Alveolar ridge crest to mandibular canal superior border distance - a clinically standard measurement in dental implant planning
pi_items.source: Dental literature: Multiple CBCT-based studies confirm ridge-to-canal distance is a standard measurement for implant site assessment. Mean bone height at first molar is 16.08±2.96 mm on dentulous sides per https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-020-73180-3

Dental literature: Research shows bone heights in mandibular posterior (first molar region) range from approximately 8-25 mm when considering both dentulous and edentulous conditions. Literature states: 'reducing the safety zone carefully, and considering the placement of short dental implants...appears to be a straightforward method for resolving intermediate atrophied mandibles' for the 8-25 mm range per https://pocketdentistry.com/10-available-bone-and-dental-implant-treatment-plans/. Mean ridge-to-canal distance at first molar: 16.08±2.96 mm (dentulous) and 13.41±2.82 mm (edentulous).

pi_items.metadata_value: real anonymized dental CBCT DICOM data from public sources
pi_items.source: Direct DICOM file inspection via pydicom: All DICOM slices contain PatientName='DENTAL^PHANTOM' and PatientID='BSP_TEST_001'. This is standard nomenclature for synthetic phantom test data, not real patient data. Verified across multiple slices (0, 25, 50, 99).

install_blueskyplan.ps1 script specifies installer URL: https://manual.blueskyplan.com/catalogs/BSP/BlueSkyPlan_5.0.29-setup64.exe. Blue Sky Plan is confirmed as legitimate dental implant planning software per https://www.blueskyplan.com/ and https://blueskybio.com/

privileged_info_summary: The measure_distance task involves using Blue Sky Plan 5.0.29 to measure the distance from the alveolar ridge crest to the mandibular canal in the lower right first molar region of a dental CBCT scan. The measurement type (ridge_to_canal_distance) is clinically valid and standard in implant planning. The expected measurement range of 8-25 mm is verified as consistent with dental literature for mandibular bone heights, where mean values at the first molar range from 13.41±2.82 mm (edentulous) to 16.08±2.96 mm (dentulous). The DICOM data used is synthetic phantom test data (PatientName='DENTAL^PHANTOM', PatientID='BSP_TEST_001'), not real patient data as claimed in the environment description.
